creator: mi+thun@aldan.algebra.com
text:
Ok, the problem here is not with the ffmpeg itself, but, rather with the 
librtmp.pc, that's installed by the rtmpdump-port: it lists packages 
libssl and libcrypto as dependencies, even though on FreeBSD there are 
no libssl.pc and libcrypto.pc installed.

The rtmpdump needs fixing like this:

    --- Makefile    23 Sep 2011 22:24:20 -0000      1.9
    +++ Makefile    11 Nov 2011 05:36:25 -0000
    @@ -8,3 +8,3 @@
      PORTVERSION=   2.3
    -PORTREVISION=  1
    +PORTREVISION=  2
      CATEGORIES=    multimedia net
    --- files/patch-librtmp-librtmp.pc.in   19 Jan 2011 01:58:24
    -0000      1.1
    +++ files/patch-librtmp-librtmp.pc.in   11 Nov 2011 05:36:25 -0000
    @@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
    ---- librtmp/librtmp.pc.in.orig 2010-07-01 04:58:34.000000000 +0900
    +--- librtmp/librtmp.pc.in      2010-07-01 04:58:34.000000000 +0900
      +++ librtmp/librtmp.pc.in      2011-01-05 23:29:34.000000000 +0900
    -@@ -8,5 +8,5 @@
    +@@ -8,5 +8,4 @@
       Version: @VERSION@
    - Requires: @CRYPTO_REQ@
    +-Requires: @CRYPTO_REQ@
       URL: http://rtmpdump.mplayerhq.hu

And after that ffmpeg itself can be improved with:

    --- Makefile    8 Nov 2011 12:05:34 -0000       1.140
    +++ Makefile    11 Nov 2011 05:37:25 -0000
    @@ -271,4 +271,5 @@
      .if defined(WITH_RTMP)
      LIB_DEPENDS+=  rtmp.0:${PORTSDIR}/multimedia/rtmpdump
    +BUILD_DEPENDS=  rtmpdump>=2.3_2:${PORTSDIR}/multimedia/rtmpdump
      CONFIGURE_ARGS+=       --enable-librtmp
      .else

And then, perhaps, RTMP-support can be enabled by default in ffmpeg... 
Please, commit the above ASAP or allow me to. Thank you very much. Yours,

    -mi
